title: Field-Guide Outcomes, Command Language, and REPL Contract · Go Semantics Case: Pokedex Go Accessible Terminal Contract
summary: A ranger team asks for a Pokedex but has not defined who uses it, which decisions it supports, what commands exist, how sessions end, or what evidence makes the command useful. Predict the user decision, Go ownership, command state, API contract, resource budget, failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the pokedex go accessible terminal contract increment in bounded field-guide product and REPL charter. Learners progress through pre…
objectives: Keep prompts, progress, errors, and results understandable without color, animation, pointer input, or fragile terminal width.
Model start, prompt, read, parse, dispatch, result, continue, EOF, quit, cancellation, and cleanup as explicit session states.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Pokedex product engineering and Go Predict Pokedex Go Accessible Terminal Contract · Theory Pokedex…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 7591 during a race-instrumented cache stress case. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate a useful, bounded, accessible REPL contract on observable command and lifecycle evidence; the release claim must identify the exact source and artifact revision; then inject one cache, waiter, goroutine, cancellation, or queue schedule and retain ownership evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original fixtures only. Real PokéAPI traffic, DNS, sockets, TLS, host files, terminals, signals, race and leak claims, load, cross-platform packaging, accessibility review, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.
